element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundation for all subsequent enemy encounters
Recurring throughout game as cannon fodder
Progressive difficulty with character name/identity changes across regions (Blood/Boots, Genocide/Scumocide)

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Stage structure evident from naming conventions
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Game organized into at least 7 numbered stages with escalating difficulty
Regional character name variations suggest differentiated movesets (Jennety/Mack, Sho/Ginzu, Hoover/Baby)

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Linear arcade-style progression with escalating difficulty toward Stage 7 final boss (Scumocide/Genocide)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to basic combat mechanics and standard enemies (Stages 1-2)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progressive boss encounters with increasing complexity (Stages 3-5)
difficulty_curve.late_game: Final stage confrontation with renamed final boss (Stage 7: Scumocide/Genocide)

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Stage-based progression with named boss encounters
notable_level_patterns.description: Seven distinct stages, each culminating in a boss fight. Bosses have region-specific names (Blood/Boots at Stage 7, Genocide/Scumocide as final boss). Generic enemies (Wooky) populate stages between bosses.
Four playable characters with regionally-altered names (Jennety→Mack, Sho→Ginzu, Hoover→Baby, plus one character), suggesting varied playstyles or special abilities per character.
SNES port of 1991 arcade game with Western censorship applied: character name changes, enemy name changes (Nooky→Wooky to remove sexual connotations), boss renamings.

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (Captain Commando SNES page)
